dialog_snippet: ...and I was able to earn a 4.0 GPA.
Absolutely...
Sweetheart, a flower can't bloom if it doesn't have roots in the dirt.
A job applicant. I see.
All we want is for you to grow and really bloom, Marzi.
Although...a sale you say...
...AND THERE WAS AN AMAZING SALE!
Am...am I dreaming?!
A month ago, I did the impossible.
And for heaven's sake, don't you dare touch a single utensil until you have earned the right.
And I don't have to go live in the woods!
And I don't know If I would have chosen THAT outfit for introductions with a prospective employer...
I remember reading that in the world of high-end confectionery, the layout is less like a standard workspace and more like a precision laboratory or a choreographed workshop.
...and I think you kind of catastrophized that whole tent in the woods scenario...
...and Marzi can tell she is being sized up in ways she doesn't comprehend.
...And my entire legacy crumbles.
And obviously Madame Sweets knows what she's doing.
And you weren't growing all alone up in that attic.
Any confectioner who knows their salt from their sugar knows that...
A pleasure to meet you.
The floors are mopped so thoroughly you could eat spun sugar right off the marble, but I think my arms have turned into jelly.
A rarity in today's youth.
A real routine.
You'll need to purchase refills for that, but you can buy them though me at a discount since I buy them in bulk.
You’re late, but at least you’re still upright!
She turns off the faucet and heads back up to her bedroom in the attic.
Beatrix gestures sharply towards the overstuffed portfolio, which has a few rogue formula sketches sticking out of the zipper.
(Back at the Glacé home, Marzi is struggling to tape a box stuffed with her leftover boutique candies).
Hold.
I am Madame Beatrix Von Sweets, owner and operator of this humble establishment.
Beatrix sighs, rubbing her temples as if fighting a chronic headache.
BE RIGHT THERE DAD!
You must have a very bold palette.
...but I could stay in the zone for ten hours straight if there was a sugar sculpture on the line.
But I did it!!
But I didn't think it was literally magical!
But I do think my customers would enjoy your style...
...but I have.
But I'm actually not a customer.
But I'm not.
AL!
But remember Marzi-
But that's where the legendary confectionery, The Gilded Macaron is.
But what if I can't?
B...But what if my portfolio isn't good enough?
But you're right, Dad.
We love your passion, but your 'research' drained your entire savings...
...candy?
CANDY!!! I WANT CANDY TOO MARZI!!
She finds the app Chai installed for her last semester.
Come on.
Just the right size for an up and coming young lady, right?
Consider it an investment in your roots.
Daddy! I'm dying! Help me!
The rent is paid every 10 days through the online portal.
Marzi. Please tell me the decimal point on this statement is an error!
Did I do something wrong?
Did you really spend your entire graduation fund on...
Do step right in, please!
Do you really think you can work to the high standards required to keep The Gilded Macaron's flawless reputation?
If you don't have a paying position by the end of the month...
Even so! This is the opportunity of a lifetime!
Everyone just calls me Marzi though.
Everything is happening really fast...
Go get 'em, confectionery genius.
Green is lightning, white is hail, you get the picture.
Have you been to Starlight City before?
HELP!
HELP ME!
Her eyes bore into the core of Marzi's soul...
Hiya! Nice to meetcha!!
The display cases need daily cleaning.
I’m actually doing it! I’m holding my own.
How am I supposed to revolutionize the confectionery world if I don't study the perfect matcha ganache?
However the copper cauldrons require hand-polishing.
Oh wow, that is such a tempting offer Madame Sweets.
I brought my portfolio and my confectionery school diploma!
I can be...
I can be an adult...
I can be disciplined...
I can do this!
I can't wait to tell Chai!
I can't wait to make something like that.
I DON'T EVEN OWN A TENT!
I'd say i'm surprised you spent all that money on candy...
I feel so small right now...what happened to make her switch like that?
If you are late, I will find another applicant.
Sometimes doing this kind of work doesn't feel great right away, and you don't have to force yourself to feel positive about it.
If you want to stand a chance at surviving in my prep room-
I got carried away trying to perfect my portfolio.
I graduated confectionery trade school with a 4.0 GPA!
I hope she's not going to be a nightmare boss...
I just... need a little inspiration first...
I knew The Gilded Macaron was supposed to be legendary...
I...I know I look a little scattered, but I'm a fast learner, and I promise I'll work hard!
I know I promised Mom and Dad that I could do this...
I'll be living in a tent in the woods!!!
I...I'll just-
I'll look in to rentals near the shop.
I'll prove to you that I have what it takes to be a world class confectioner!
I'm 22 years old...
I'm also very creative and i have so many ideas for-
I'm going to take the best bubble baths ever after work!
I'm having a heart attack!
I'm Marzipan Glace. Marzi for short.
I'm nervous about my portfolio quality so I was sniffing out the competition...
I'm not dreaming and I'm not dead!
I'm not looking for my junior staff to reinvent the wheel.
Impeccable fashion sense as well I see.
I must of course then recommend our house made macarons.
In through the nose...2...3...4...
I personally oversee all the enchantments on our macarons.
I really did it!!
I run a tight ship around here, Ms. Glace.
I saw the job posting about an opening in the confectionery.
-is waiting for me to fail.
I thought the hard part was over.
I thought you moved away!
The city is full of handsome young makers and artisans, you know.
It's even better than I'd imagined!
It's not just candy, Dad!
It's research!
It's so intricate...
It's time to put that preparation into action.
It's...well, it's going to be a lot of floor scrubbing at first.
It was awful! A whirlwind of mop buckets and cold stares, Dad.
It's your big chance!
I was officially a real confectioner...
I was the kind of student who needed a map to find the walk-in freezer...
I will mold you like these chocolate sculptures, Ms. Glace, from the bottom molecule to the top.
I won't let you down!
Let me stop you right there.
Let's do three deep breaths together, okay?
Look at me please Marzi.
Look at that carousel made of isomalt!
Look, if you're going to blow your entire life savings on sugar, you might as well go straight to the source.
Madame Von Sweets is...intense.
Maddy, please eat the peas and you can have purin after.
(Madeline scurries past, giggling as she successfully sneaks a rock-candy sample from Marzi’s open pocket).
Many of them come in after shopping at the boutique next door.
Marzi drops her fork, her eyes widen to saucers.
Marzi tries to hide her stained wrist behind her back.
She looks Marzi up and down one last time, an exhausted resignation in her eyes.
MARZIPAN! COME DOWNSTAIRS THIS INSTANT!
Marzi gulps, pressure building on her chest.
Marzi, I'm pretty sure Mom and Dad just said that so you'd take getting a job seriously...
Marzi opens her eyes before the second ring, reaching out to tap the snooze button with a steady hand.
Marzi's confidence starts to falter.
Marzi lets out a shaky breath, her shoulders dropping an inch.
Marzi swallows hard, the crushing weight of Beatrix's cold stare flashing in her mind.
Marzi! What on earth is going on?
My methods can be somewhat... unconventional, but the proof is in the pudding, Madame Sweets...
Might I recommend our salted soft caramels?
Mom and I agreed on a timeline, Marzi.
MOM! DAD!
Mommy! My chest is tight!
You've been locked up in your bedroom for months...
And my birthday is on February 29-
My blood type is AB...
M...My chest is feeling tight...
My name is Marzipan Glace.
My, what a confident girl you are!
No blowing your paycheck on imported candy.
No giving up because it's hard or you're having trouble with a coworker...
No...I..I haven't.
No impulsive quitting...
No, I must have died on the bus ride and gone to heaven!!
No, no... nevermind.
No one has a passion for confectionery arts more intense than you.
...normal.
NO TOUCH!
Now, quit complaining Marzi!
No, you must first prove you are even capable of the basics.
Thank you!
Okay, Marzi.
Omigosh WOW!
Before I decide to reassign you to scrubbing the caramel vats for the rest of the week.
One bad review on SweetSnap...
...One misfired enchantment...
Ouch!
Our in house confectioners have have enchanted them so they don't stick to your teeth!
Our in house confectioners have enchanted them so that you feel the chill of falling snowflakes when you take a bite.
PURIN! PURIN! JIGGLY PURIN!
You must have a very refined palette.
Right.
I understand...
It is ridiculous that my earnings are being cut just because the new girl was in the way.
She doesn't want me touching the cooking utensils until I "prove myself"...
She snatches her cellphone off the ground and glares at Marzi, her dark eyes narrowing.
Plus we saw the photos of the unit and it was simply too perfect to pass up.
Slowly, the tightness begins to unravel like a knot finally loosening.
He flawlessly glides the mop in a smooth, continuous figure-eight over the tile floor of the closet.
The rhythm of her breath finally syncs with the pulsing light on her screen.
She taps her cell phone screen.
Since you're working downtown now, the commute from the attic isn't really practical anymore.
So don't blow it...
So much pressure...
Hey.
So this is the legendary Gilded Macaron...
So you just do as she says.
Thanks, guys.
The walk to the bus stop is a miserable blur of cold wind and exhaustion.
That means 15 minutes early, Ms. Glace.
That's kind of...weird, right?
That was sooo much harder than I thought it would be!
Oh wow! That would be my dream job!
The blue ones tastes like a summer shower and you don't need a drink to wash it down.
The glass displays are clean, the floor is spotless and the day is done.
The marble floors need scrubbing.
Then it's settled.
Honestly, if this is the standard of help we are hiring now, my followers are going to notice the drop in quality.
There is powdered sugar on your left shoe.
There's a new listing for a job opening.
There's so much riding on me doing well...
There is even enough counter space for a small marble tempering slab!
Ugh, the humidity from those tempering vats is an absolute nightmare today.
The volcano that exploded rainbow sprinkles in the principal's face?
The wheel, which as you can see, is rolling flawlessly the way it is.
The world out there-
Marzi looks at the empty floor, then up at Huck, who is quietly wringing out his own towel.
They'll like you Marzi.
You must have a very eclectic palette.
This is a great opportunity.
This is real.
THIS IS REEEEAL!!!
Chai's right... it's just physics... it's just chemistry.
I thought the graduation money would be my ticket to my own confectionery.
Today's theme is weather, so the yellow macarons taste like sundew-nice for warming up in the evening before bed.
To Marzi!
Uh oh...
Be sure to make the most of your time in the city after work.
(She slumps against the doorframe and a virtual pet beeps a low-battery warning from her bag).
Waaaah!
Wait, really?
We can't keep funding this chaos.
We have an extra fresh batch of turkish delights that I think you'll love.
We hope having your own "pot" will give you some space to flourish.
We knew you just needed a little push.
Welcome! Welcome to The Gilded Macaron, my dear!
Well aren't you such an... interesting girl!
...well...kind of...
Well you might not have been searching the job boards...
...we are going to kick you out of the house, Marzipan.
We are so proud of you, sweetheart!
We rotate the flavor profiles and effects out every day.
We shall see about that, Ms. Glace...
-We will start by infusing discipline in you like seeding a boiling syrup.
What do I do??
What if I let everyone down?
What if I let myself down...?
What if they don't like me??
What was up with that lady though?
Which makes me a pisces!
Who managed to land a job at the most prestigious confectionery in Starlight City on her very first try!
Oh, and fair warning, Madame Sweets enchants all of the surfaces in the confectionery.
(Looking up from a financial ledger with a firm, practical expression, his reading glasses perched on the end of his nose).
Would you like to try a sample before you purchase, young miss?
Y...yes Dad?
Yes, Ma'am!
Yes, Madame Sweets!
You also have ink-or perhaps food coloring-smudged on your wrist.
You'd really do that?!
You have to hold this job down to pay the monthly rent.
You have to prove you can follow the recipe of true success.
You're...messy...you lack focus...
You need a steady job.
You're looking for someone more experienced...
Here is your daily pay of 1000 gold pieces. Deflecting blame has cost you any chance of earning a bonus.
Your mom and I have decided to cover the security deposit and first month's rent for an apartment near the shop.
Your presentation is chaotic at best.
R...Right.
Your shift starts at 5AM tomorrow morning.
You've been spending more time shopping for sweets, outfits and fidget toys than you have been applying for jobs.
You've prepared enough, okay?
You won't find another comparable.
